food_subgroup: Hot beverage sachet gift set — instant coffee and tea sachets

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a multi-item sachet gift set combining instant coffee and tea. FOODON_03301710 (coffee beverage) is used as the closest parent for the coffee component. The tea component is not separately coded here.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Coffee and coffee substitutes) is the closest parent for the dominant coffee sachet component. No FoodEx2 code exists for a bundled coffee-and-tea sachet retail gift set.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps to a retail sachet gift set of this type. SNOMED CT does not cover retail food bundles at this granularity.

preparation_method: instant — dissolve sachet in hot water
main_ingredients: instant coffee powder (Robusta or Arabica blend)
allergens: milk derivatives (non-dairy creamer may contain casein)

serving.natural_language_portion: One sachet (approximately 25 g) dissolved in 150–180 ml of hot water constitutes a single serving of either the kopi or the Straits Classic Tea.

nutrient_source.title: Instant 3-in-1 Coffee (Kopi) — USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods; supplemented with HPB Singapore nutrient data for local kopi sachet products
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists in any approved source for the Toast Box Kopi Sachet or Straits Classic Tea sachet. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entries for instant 3-in-1 coffee powder (NDB 14209 / FDC ID 171891) and HPB Singapore data for sweetened instant coffee beverages, adapted to a per-100 g dry-powder basis. The set contains two boxes of kopi sachets and one box of Straits Classic Tea; nutrients represent the dry sachet powder averaged across both product types. Values should be treated as estimates.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sweetened instant coffee and tea beverages — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for instant coffee with sugar and milk (GI ~60–70) and sweetened tea (GI ~60–65)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Toast Box Kopi Sachet or Straits Classic Tea sachet. GI of 65 is estimated as the midpoint of the range for sweetened instant coffee (GI ~60–70) and sweetened tea (GI ~60–65) as listed in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (65 × 7.4) / 100 ≈ 4.8, where available carbohydrate per 25 g sachet serving = 76 g × 0.25 × 0.39 ≈ 7.4 g (accounting for dilution in ~150 ml water giving ~175 g total beverage, carbohydrate contribution from sachet powder only).

hypertension.guidance: Each kopi sachet contributes approximately 30 mg sodium per prepared cup; caffeine in Robusta-based kopi may transiently raise blood pressure, so individuals with hypertension should limit intake to one to two cups per day and choose lower-sugar variants where available.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content from non-dairy creamer and added sugar in sachet formulation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Each 25 g kopi sachet delivers approximately 13–14 g of added sugar when prepared as directed, contributing meaningfully to daily glycaemic load; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should use unsweetened or reduced-sugar kopi sachets and monitor postprandial glucose responses carefully.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from non-dairy creamer (palm-oil-based) and high sugar load
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The non-dairy creamer in standard kopi sachets is typically palm-oil-based and contributes saturated fat (~1.6 g per serving); combined with high sugar content, regular consumption of multiple sachets daily may adversely affect lipid profiles and cardiovascular risk in susceptible individuals.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Toast Box Kopi Sachet and Straits Classic Tea Collection Set is a retail gift bundle sold by Toast Box, a Singaporean café chain specialising in traditional Nanyang (Southeast Asian Chinese) coffee culture. The set comprises two boxes of instant kopi (Nanyang-style coffee) sachets of the customer's choice and one box of Straits Classic Tea sachets. Kopi is a distinctively Singaporean beverage made from Robusta coffee beans roasted with butter and sugar, producing a rich, bold, slightly caramelised flavour. It is traditionally served with sweetened condensed milk or evaporated milk. In sachet form, the kopi powder is pre-blended with non-dairy creamer and sugar, requiring only the addition of hot water. Straits Classic Tea is a blend of black tea with a mild, smooth character inspired by traditional Straits Chinese tea culture. Each sachet is dissolved in 150–180 ml of hot water to produce a single serving. Nutritionally, the dry sachet powder is energy-dense (~385 kcal per 100 g) due to its high sugar (~55 g/100 g) and non-dairy creamer content, though a prepared cup from one 25 g sachet delivers approximately 96 kcal. The high sugar content is a primary concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 65 (medium) based on analogous sweetened instant coffee and tea entries in the University of Sydney GI Database. This product is culturally significant as a representation of Singapore's Nanyang coffee heritage and is commonly purchased as a gift or souvenir.
common_user_queries: What is in the Toast Box Collection Set?
How many sachets are in the Toast Box kopi set?
How much sugar is in a kopi sachet?
Is kopi sachet suitable for people with high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: No exact nutrient entry exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central) for the Toast Box Kopi Sachet or Straits Classic Tea sachet products specifically. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central instant 3-in-1 coffee entries and HPB Singapore data for sweetened instant coffee beverages. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. This is a retail gift bundle, not a single food item; nutrient values represent the dry sachet powder averaged across the kopi and tea components on a per-100 g basis. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central data for comparable instant coffee sachet products; non-dairy creamer formulations in Singapore have largely eliminated trans fats. 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as the product contains no animal-derived fat (non-dairy creamer is plant-based). 4. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero; coffee and tea sachets of this type contain no vitamin C. 5. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data for instant coffee sachets. 6. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 7. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 8. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 9. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 10. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 11. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 12. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 13. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 14. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 15. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 16. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 17. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 18. GI set to 65 (medium) as an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for sweetened instant coffee and sweetened tea; no direct measurement exists for this product. 19. Ontology codes are closest-parent matches only; no exact ontology term exists for a bundled sachet gift set of this type. 20. Price SGD 39.00 per set as listed on field survey data (Toast Box – Funan outlet). 21. Allergen information is indicative; consumers should verify with Toast Box directly as formulations may vary.
